articles.title: APPLE REFINES LIQUID GLASS DESIGN AND EXPANDS CHILD SAFETY TOOLS AT WWDC
articles.summary: Apple refined its Liquid Glass design language at WWDC 2026 with improved readability, contrast, and user controls for transparency levels. The company announced iOS 27 performance improvements, including faster app launches, photo loading, and network transitions. Apple significantly expanded its child safety tools with enhanced parental controls for managing children's device usage, screen time, and communication access.

articles.title: AUDI UNVEILS SECRET SUPERCAR, NEW DESIGN DIRECTION
articles.summary: Audi's Chief Creative Officer, Massimo Frascella, has unveiled the Nuvolari supercar, representing the brand's new "Radical Next" design direction focused on radical simplicity. The hybrid vehicle combines a 4-liter twin-turbo V8 with three electric motors to produce nearly 1,000 horsepower. Only 499 units will be produced at over $650,000 each.

articles.title: APPLE'S PHOTOS APP IS GETTING THREE NEW AI-POWERED EDITING TOOLS
articles.summary: iOS 27 introduces three AI-powered photo editing upgrades in the Photos app: a more capable Cleanup tool for removing unwanted objects, Extend for expanding a photo beyond its original frame using generative AI, and Reframe, which uses spatial data and AI to reposition subjects and adjust composition after the photo is taken. All three features are powered by Apple Intelligence and are expected to arrive later this year.

articles.title: DIGITAL SKILLS AND TECH TRENDS: WHAT DESIGNERS MUST MASTER NOW
articles.summary: Creative professionals must now blend traditional design skills with technical and AI expertise, as 69% of marketing leaders say AI is reshaping — not replacing — the skills their teams need. The four most in-demand design roles are UX design, product design, front-end development, and visual design. Beyond hard skills, cross-functional collaboration and clear digital communication are what ultimately land the job, since technical ability draws attention but interpersonal skills close the deal.

articles.title: WCAG COMPLIANCE LEVELS EXPLAINED: A, AA, AND AAA
articles.summary: WCAG defines three conformance levels — A, AA, and AAA — each building on the previous, with Level AA being the standard most organizations are expected to meet. Although WCAG itself is not a law, Level AA is widely treated as the legal benchmark, with US ADA enforcement and EU directives commonly referencing WCAG 2.1 or 2.2 at that level. Level AAA, the most rigorous tier, is generally treated as an aspirational target applied selectively rather than a universal requirement.

articles.title: THIS YEAR'S WWDC REVEALED SOMETHING SURPRISING ABOUT APPLE'S FUTURE
articles.summary: At WWDC, Apple announced that the long-delayed AI-powered Siri, now called Siri AI, will launch in September with its own dedicated app, fulfilling promises first made two years ago. The keynote also marked a major shift in presentation style, with Apple discussing software updates across all devices together rather than separating them by platform. The change led some viewers to feel confused about platform-specific updates, but it may reflect Apple's growing focus on a unified software ecosystem. By treating iPhone, iPad, Mac, and other devices as parts of a single experience, Apple appears to be moving toward a more integrated vision for its operating systems.

articles.title: 10M+ WINNING ADS, ALL IN ONE PLACE
articles.summary: Hooksy is a platform that provides access to over 10 million winning ads and allows marketers to track competitors' creative strategies in real-time. The tool consolidates ad discovery, saving, and tracking into one workspace with features like automated brand monitoring, one-click ad saving, and AI script extraction.

articles.title: SKELETON SCREENS FOR YOUR UI
articles.summary: Boneyard is a UI tool that provides skeleton screens for applications. The library helps create loading placeholders that improve user experience during content loading.

articles.title: CREATE ANY APP IN MINUTES BY CHATTING WITH AI
articles.summary: Glaze by Raycast allows users to create desktop apps in minutes by chatting with AI, requiring no coding knowledge. The apps run locally on your machine with OS integration, accessing files and tools while keeping data private.

articles.title: HOW TO USE CULTURAL PROBES TO UNDERSTAND YOUR USERS
articles.summary: Cultural probes are research tools that help designers understand users when direct observation isn't possible or when deeper insights are needed beyond what web conferencing and video recording can provide. They allow researchers to dig deeper into problem domains and contexts of use that technological solutions cannot fully capture. Cultural probes serve as an alternative method to gain detailed understanding of users and improve design decisions.

articles.title: TOP UX DESIGN TRENDS: HOW USER EXPERIENCE DESIGN IS EVOLVING IN 2026
articles.summary: UX design is evolving toward more intentional, personal approaches that prioritize user-centered experiences over generic layouts. Key trends include "slow browsing" that reduces stimulation and gives products more breathing room, plus high-performance interfaces where seamless micro-interactions signal brand quality. Brands are moving away from homogeneous designs toward more textured, analog-inspired interfaces that feel hyper-unique and immersive.

articles.title: WEDGE DESIGNS STONE&SKILLET'S IDENTITY AROUND THE PAN IT'S NAMED AFTER
articles.summary: Stone&Skillet worked with Wedge to refresh its brand identity as it expanded into thousands of stores. The redesign centers on a simplified skillet symbol, bolder typography, richer colors, and more distinctive photography, creating a stronger shelf presence while preserving the brand's warmth and craftsmanship. The new system is designed to be flexible and scalable, helping the brand stand out in a crowded category and supporting future product growth.

articles.title: FIVE BRILLIANTLY WEIRD 3D PRINTED DESIGNS THAT SHOW EXACTLY WHERE INDUSTRIAL DESIGN IS HEADED
articles.summary: 3D printing is transforming industrial design by turning standardized tech products into customized, ergonomic, and expressive forms that better serve human needs.

articles.title: SIX COMMON FONT PAIRING MISTAKES AND HOW TO AVOID THEM
articles.summary: Common font pairing mistakes include using overly similar typefaces, neglecting defined roles, pairing two expressive display fonts, combining serifs without expertise, ignoring typographic hierarchy, and unnecessarily using multiple typefaces at all.

articles.title: DOWNLOAD THE OFFICIAL MACOS 27 GOLDEN GATE WALLPAPERS HERE
articles.summary: Apple has released the new macOS 27 Golden Gate wallpapers ahead of launch.
